title: Dividend Yield Calculator: Annual Income Estimator
description: Calculate expected annual dividend income, project yield-on-cost, and estimate portfolio growth based on real market data with our interactive calculator.

schemas.headline: Dividend Yield Calculator: Annual Income Estimator
schemas.description: Calculate expected annual dividend income, project yield-on-cost, and estimate portfolio growth based on real market data.

acceptedAnswer.text: Dividend yield is calculated by dividing the annual dividend paid per share by the current share price, then multiplying by 100 to express it as a percentage. For example, if a company pays $2 annually and trades at $50, the yield is 4%.

Yield on cost represents your annual dividend income divided by your original investment amount. If a company raises its dividend over time, your yield on cost increases even if the current market yield stays the same.

mainEntity.name: How does dividend growth affect income?

acceptedAnswer.text: When companies consistently raise their dividends, your annual income grows organically without requiring additional capital investment. This compounding effect significantly boosts long-term total returns.
No. Dividends are declared by a company's board of directors and can be cut or suspended if the company faces financial difficulties or decides to retain cash.

mainEntity.name: Why do high dividend yields sometimes indicate risk?

acceptedAnswer.text: A very high yield can sometimes be a 'dividend trap,' indicating that the market expects the dividend to be cut, causing the stock price to drop significantly. Always review the payout ratio and earnings history.
